description: Richer portfolio metrics in daily paper-trading reports — GitHub trovlunde/stock_analyzer#7

userStories.title: Add empyrical and portfolio metrics module
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want Calmar, Sortino, and rolling Sharpe computed via empyrical so daily reports stop relying on hand-rolled math.
acceptanceCriteria: `uv add empyrical`; import succeeds in CI
New module (e.g. `stock_analysis/trading/portfolio_metrics.py`) exposes `compute_portfolio_metrics(returns, *, rolling_window=63)` returning Calmar, Sortino, and rolling Sharpe
Uses empyrical functions — not duplicated Sharpe/drawdown formulas from legacy `ai/metrics.py`
Handles short series gracefully (returns None or empty for metrics when insufficient data)
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: PortfolioStore equity curve from completed runs
userStories.description: As the daily job, I want ordered historical portfolio values from `paper_runs` summaries so metrics can be computed on an equity curve.
acceptanceCriteria: `PortfolioStore` gains a read method returning ordered `(session_date, portfolio_value)` from completed non–dry-run runs for a portfolio
Parses `summary_json` fields `session_date` and `portfolio_value` written by `run_daily`
Excludes dry-run runs and runs without `finished_at` / valid summary
Unit test uses in-memory SQLite fixture with mocked run rows (no network)
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: Portfolio metrics section in daily markdown report
userStories.description: As a paper-trading user, I want Calmar, Sortino, and rolling Sharpe in `data/reports/YYYY-MM-DD.md` when enough history exists.
acceptanceCriteria: `write_daily_report` accepts optional portfolio metrics dict and renders a `## Portfolio metrics` section
Section lists Calmar, Sortino, and latest rolling Sharpe (or summary stat) with sensible formatting
When metrics unavailable (insufficient history), section notes insufficient history instead of bogus values
Existing fills/signals sections unchanged
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: Wire daily job to compute and pass metrics
userStories.description: As the daily job, I want to build an equity curve after each session and pass computed metrics to the report writer without changing broker or decision logic.
acceptanceCriteria: `run_daily` loads equity curve via new store method, appends current `total_value` for today
Calls `compute_portfolio_metrics` on daily returns derived from equity values
Passes metrics to `write_daily_report` on normal trading-day path
Dry-run and NYSE-closed paths unchanged in broker/decision behavior; may omit metrics or show note
`PaperBroker`, `decide`, and order queueing logic untouched
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: P1 pytest: known returns and report integration
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want regression tests on synthetic data proving metric accuracy and report output without network or live yfinance.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: fixed synthetic daily return series → Calmar, Sortino, rolling Sharpe within tolerance vs empyrical reference
Test: `write_daily_report` with metrics dict produces markdown containing `## Portfolio metrics` and expected values
Tests use fixtures/mocks only — no live DB or yfinance
Existing `tests/trading/test_paper_broker.py` and `tests/trading/test_decision.py` still pass
uv run pytest passes
